title: Paper leak row: Education minister announces computer-based NEET exams from next year
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan announced NEET UG exams will be computer-based from next year, emphasizing zero tolerance for malpractices amid paper leak allegations. A re-examination is scheduled for June 21st, with the investigation handed over to the CBI to address the examination mafia and ensure fairness for deserving students.

title: India signs MoU on strategic petroleum, LPG supplies during PM Modi’s UAE visit
content: The two sides also signed a memorandum of understanding related to strategic petroleum reserves, along with an agreement concerning supplies of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG).

title: Hardik Pandya likely to return for KKR clash, confirms MI teammate
content: Mumbai Indians' captain Hardik Pandya is sidelined with a back spasm, missing three consecutive matches. All-rounder Shardul Thakur provided an update, suggesting Pandya could return for the May 20th match against Kolkata Knight Riders. Despite the team's elimination from IPL 2026, Pandya's absence and poor form have been significant factors in their disappointing season.

title: Fuel gets costlier: Centre raised petrol, diesel prices by Rs 3 - key details
content: Fuel prices saw a Rs 3 per litre increase on Friday. This move follows significant losses for state-run oil companies due to soaring global crude oil prices. The conflict in West Asia disrupted key shipping routes, pushing up import costs for India. This price adjustment comes after a period of frozen rates, impacting transportation and other sectors.

title: Oracle revokes job offers made to students of IIT & other biggest colleges - here's why
content: Tech giant Oracle has reportedly rescinded dozens of job offers made to students at India's top engineering colleges, including IITs and NITs. This move follows recent mass layoffs and has caused significant anxiety among students as placement seasons conclude. Affected students are now actively seeking new opportunities.

title: 'Arshdeep should be banned': Ex-India star fumes over racist remark on Tilak Varma
content: Punjab Kings bowler Arshdeep Singh faces backlash after a video surfaced showing him allegedly making a racially insensitive remark to Mumbai Indians batter Tilak Varma. Former India spinner Laxman Sivaramakrishnan strongly condemned the comment, recalling his own experiences with skin tone-based mockery and demanding strict action against Arshdeep.

title: Unnao rape case: SC quashes Delhi HC order suspending Kuldeep Singh Sengar's life term
content: The Supreme Court has overturned the Delhi High Court's suspension of former BJP MLA Kuldeep Singh Sengar's life sentence in the Unnao rape case. The apex court has ordered the High Court to re-examine the matter within two months, ensuring a fresh decision free from prior influences.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: Petrol, diesel price hike: Indian Oil director calls it ‘very small rise’
content: The government on Friday raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs 3 per litre nationwide. In Delhi, petrol prices increased from Rs 94.77 to Rs 97.77 per litre, while diesel rates moved up from Rs 87.67 to Rs 90.67 per litre.

title: Price prediction: Where are gold rates headed today & what should investors do?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are seeing a weak intra-day bias, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities. He recommends a sell on rise strategy:

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: PBKS slump to 43.8% as GT and RCB tighten playoff grip
content: With playoffs in sight for some and out of reach for others, the IPL race intensifies. Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bangalore are nearing qualification, while Sunrisers Hyderabad are strong contenders. Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals hold even chances for the top four, as Punjab Kings' hopes dwindle. Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals face slim prospects.

title: As layoffs cross 1 lakh across tech industry, here are AI-proof jobs for engineers
content: Tech layoffs have surged past 1 lakh in 2026, impacting major firms like LinkedIn and Meta, even as companies invest heavily in AI. While mid-level and entry-level roles are cut, demand is rising for experienced engineers skilled in AI operations, maintenance, and integration. Soft skills and a proven AI track record are now crucial for securing these sought-after positions.

title: Delhi gets hydrogen-fueled shuttle buses: All you need to know
content: Delhi Metro Rail Corporation has launched an integrated hydrogen-powered shuttle bus service in the Central Vista area, featuring two buses with a 35-passenger capacity each. Supported by Indian Oil Corporation Limited, this initiative aims to provide an environmentally friendly last-mile connectivity option.

title: 'When I was in the air ...': UP’s ‘flying man’ survives 50ft storm lift, lands in field
content: An e-rickshaw driver was lifted nearly 50ft and flung 100 metres by storm winds after trying to secure a tin shed. He landed in a sugarcane field with fractures, his miraculous survival captured in a viral video. The incident highlighted the storm's ferocity and the man's narrow escape.

title: Hormuz crisis: India’s oil stock down 15% since US-Iran war - here’s what it means
content: Analysts believe that if supply disruptions persist for an extended period, refiners may eventually have to scale back refinery runs or reduce crude processing activity. They say this could partly explain Prime Minister Narendra Modi urging citizens to conserve fuel.

title: NEET UG exam 2026 rescheduled for June 21 after paper leak row
content: The National Testing Agency has announced a re-examination for NEET (UG) 2026 on Sunday, June 21, following the cancellation of the May 3 exam due to alleged irregularities. Investigators uncovered evidence of a "guess paper" with numerous questions closely matching the actual exam, prompting a CBI probe into the matter.

title: NIA reveals Pakistan's plan to push 'Ghazwa-e-Hind' through 'doctor-terror' module
content: NIA's chargesheet reveals Ansar Ghazwat-ul-Hind (AGuH) has evolved from a forest-based group to an urban threat, orchestrated by Pakistan-based handlers. This shift, moving from visual propaganda to a tech-driven network, aims to link Kashmiri militancy with global jihadist goals, bypassing traditional structures through encrypted platforms.

title: From Meitei-Kuki to Naga-Kuki: Conflict widens across Manipur’s hills & valley
content: The complex tensions in Manipur have surged beyond the Meitei and Kuki-Zo conflict, drawing the Naga groups into a heated fray. Retired Lt Gen Prabin Kalita posits that external players may be exploiting the turmoil to further engage the Nagas, intensifying an already volatile situation.

title: Youth late to open Ahmedabad PG facility door killed by roommates
content: A fatal dispute over a room door at a Vejalpur PG accommodation has claimed the life of 25-year-old Hiren Solanki. Two co-residents allegedly stabbed him after a heated argument escalated into a physical altercation. This incident marks the fifth murder in Ahmedabad in just four days, with police swiftly arresting the accused.

title: 'Karuppu' review: Suriya’s screen presence steals the show, fans call it a mass treat
content: Suriya's powerful presence and mass moments are electrifying audiences in 'Karuppu,' finally released after delays. Fans are raving about the interval sequences and Suriya's performance, calling it a treat. Director RJ Balaji's storytelling and the film's emotional depth are also winning praise, with strong social media buzz indicating a promising theatrical run.

title: Oil prices today: Crude rises over 1% as Trump warns Iran, Hormuz concerns persist
content: Global oil prices surged as President Trump expressed impatience with Iran, fueling fears of Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Brent and WTI crude saw significant weekly gains amid uncertainty over the Iran conflict and supply concerns. Despite claims of increased vessel crossings, the Strait remains a critical choke point, with maritime incidents further heightening market anxieties about tight supply.

title: 'Elections over, vasooli shuru': Oppn hits out at PM over fuel hike with 'mehangayi man' jab
content: Fuel prices have increased across India. Petrol and diesel rates have gone up by Rs 3 per litre. Opposition parties are criticising the Modi government. They state that elections are over and price recovery has begun. This hike comes amid global energy price surges. India had previously absorbed these pressures. State-owned oil companies are now adjusting prices.

title: Fuel prices up: Petrol, diesel hiked by Rs 3/litre amid global crude surge
content: The Centre has repeatedly asserted that there is no fuel shortage in the country and no plan to introduce rationing of petrol, diesel or LPG despite disruptions in global energy shipments linked to the Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

title: 'Compromised PM': Rahul Gandhi's fresh swipe at PM Modi over India-US trade deal
content: Rahul Gandhi has accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of striking a "deal for Adani's release" with the US, rather than a genuine trade agreement. The Congress party echoed these sentiments, labeling the pact "one-sided" and alleging it aims to close fraud cases against Gautam Adani in America, suggesting Modi is compromised and subservient to US demands.

title: ‘Dispute over money’: Accused in Delhi bus gangrape case tells cops; what we know so far
content: Delhi Police have apprehended the driver and conductor of a private sleeper bus. They are accused of raping a 30-year-old woman in Nangloi three days ago. The woman stated she boarded the bus late at night. The accused allegedly drove the vehicle to a secluded area and assaulted her. The accused claim a monetary dispute was involved. Investigations are ongoing.

title: PM Modi leaves for UAE - what's on his five-nation tour agenda
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi begins a significant five-nation tour. He will visit the United Arab Emirates, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, and Italy. The visits aim to boost India's engagement with Europe and the Gulf region. Discussions will focus on trade, investment, energy, and technology. This tour underscores India's growing global partnerships.

title: 117 killed as storms lash UP with cyclone-like 130kmph winds
content: A severe thunderstorm and squall ravaged Uttar Pradesh on Wednesday, claiming 117 lives and injuring 79. Violent storms, collapsing structures, and lightning strikes caused widespread destruction, with Prayagraj being the worst-hit district. High wind speeds, reaching nearly cyclonic levels, uprooted trees and damaged hundreds of houses and livestock.

title: ‘Xi would also like to see the Hormuz Strait open’: Trump on US-China talks
content: US President Trump revealed Chinese President Xi Jinping desires the Strait of Hormuz reopened to stabilize global energy markets. Both leaders reportedly agreed on the necessity of this crucial shipping route's resumption. Discussions also touched upon potential increased Chinese purchases of American oil and agricultural products, alongside ongoing tensions regarding Taiwan.

title: Check latest petrol, diesel rates in your city and state after Rs 3 increase
content: Fuel prices have surged across India, with petrol now exceeding Rs 110 per litre in several states and diesel nearing Rs 100 in many regions. This hike, averaging Rs 3 per litre, follows sustained pressure from elevated global crude oil prices, exacerbated by the West Asia crisis.

title: From doctrine to dominance: how integrated systems shaped Operation Sindoor
content: Outgoing CDS General Anil Chauhan highlighted India's success in Operation Sindoor, attributing it to integrated systems and superior situational awareness, leading to a non-kinetic victory. He stressed the need for multi-domain warfare readiness and cultural shifts for effective theatre commands, acknowledging a decade-long capability gap in implementation.

title: 'Torturous for boxers': Drained Nikhat Zareen suffers shock trials exit
content: Two-time world champion Nikhat Zareen faced a shocking defeat in boxing trials, missing out on CWG and Asian Games. She revealed the immense physical and mental strain caused by a chaotic two-week preparation, citing administrative tussles between BFI and SAI as a major factor. Despite the setback, Zareen plans to focus on rebuilding her fitness for the LA Olympics.

title: 2 men rape orphaned girls aged 11 and 12 for over a year in Bengaluru, post videos online
content: Two men have been arrested in Bengaluru for sexually abusing two minor girls, recording the acts, and uploading them online for profit. The arrests followed an alert from the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.

title: 'You wouldn't find a guy like him in Hollywood': Trump praises China's Xi Jinping
content: Donald Trump praised Chinese President Xi Jinping, calling him a respected leader governing nearly 1.5 billion people and suggesting Hollywood couldn't find someone like him. Trump agreed with Xi's assessment of the US as a declining nation, attributing it to the Biden administration's policies, while highlighting his own administration's economic successes.

title: Groom not the man she met on Instagram, bride calls off wedding in UP
content: A wedding in Shahabad descended into chaos when the bride alleged the groom was not the man she knew online. The man, identified as Devendra Singh Parmar, claimed he was the one the bride had been speaking to. Police have registered an FIR against 10 people for cheating and conspiracy, arresting five, including the groom.

title: 'I don't see that happening': Bumrah on ODI captaincy after MI win
content: Jasprit Bumrah captained Mumbai Indians for the first time in IPL 2026, leading them to a six-wicket victory against Punjab Kings. This leadership role came in the absence of regular captains Hardik Pandya and Suryakumar Yadav. Bumrah, who has captained India in Tests and T20Is, expressed his enjoyment of the experience and praised the team's execution.

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Bajaj Broking Research has chosen NMDC, and Jayaswal Neco Industries as the top stock picks for May 15, 2026. The brokerage has shared its target prices and time period to attain it. Let’s take a look:

title: RPI(Athavale) leader stabbed to death at Pune bar
content: Republican Party of India (Athavale)'s Kaamgar Aghadi state executive president Anil Maruti More was brutally stabbed to death by two assailants at a Moshi bar. The murder is believed to be a fallout of personal enmity, with police investigating CCTV footage and other clues to apprehend the suspects.

title: BRICS consensus on West Asia uncertain as Iran, UAE spar at meeting
content: BRICS foreign ministers are struggling to agree on a joint statement concerning West Asia. Tensions flared between the UAE and Iran during the meeting. Iran accused the UAE of direct involvement in aggression. India's External Affairs Minister stressed the importance of consensus for new members. The outcome of the meeting remains uncertain.

title: Census 2027: How to do self-enumeration - a step-by-step guide
content: Census 2027 will be completely digital, with enumerators recording data from house visits on mobile applications.

title: Watch: Inside Vijay’s ‘Leo’-inspired cafe in Kashmir
content:
Sifar Cafe in Anantnag district, 40 kilometres from Pahalgam in South Kashmir, has become the epicentre of tourists from South India. It is the exact location where the new Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay’s 2023 hit film Leo was shot.
In the week since he assumed the role as Chief Minister, Sifar has seen a 90% spike in tourists from Tamil Nadu. Visitors throng the location, recreating Vijay’s walk. They also order ‘chocolate coffee’, a famous device used in the film to further the plot.

title: Fuel price hike sparks public anger in Thiruvananthapuram
content: In Thiruvananthapuram, petrol is now priced at ₹110.76 per litre, while diesel has touched ₹99.64 per litre

title: NEET-UG paper leak row: Exam to go fully computer-based from next year, says Education Minister Pradhan
content: The Union Minister said that the Government would not let the malpractice happen again and that it was sensitive to the hard work that students put in for exam preparation

title: Unnao rape case: Supreme Court sets aside order suspending Kuldeep Sengar’s sentence, asks Delhi HC to consider afresh
content: The court said the High Court must hear all the parties, including the counsel for the survivor of the crime, on the question of suspension of sentence

title: Andhra government notifies recruitment drive to fill 1,523 teaching faculty posts across 19 universities
content: Applications will be accepted online from May 18 through the portal and the last date for submission is June 8

content: Amid pressure on fuel supplies, oil-marketing companies hiked petrol and diesel prices by ₹3 across all variants

title: India calls for UNSC expansion, says lack of reform ‘constrains’ global body
content: The External Affairs Minister made the remarks at a conclave of the Foreign Ministers of the BRICS nations in New Delhi

title: A mural on India’s maritime history to feature on the walls of the Kochi Naval Base
content: A collaborative project by the Better Kochi Response Group, Regional Sports Centre and the Indian Navy, the Kochi Naval wall art will portray India’s sealinks from ancient times to modern day

title: Kerala politics LIVE: CM-elect Satheesan meets Pinarayi Vijayan; UDF leadership meet to decide Kerala's Cabinet
content: Senior Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala said that the CM has the prerogative to decide the composition of the Cabinet

content: The public statement also alleged that on April 23 the family met Union Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar at his residence in the hope of reassurance and protection

title: Gautam Adani agrees to $18 million penalty in U.S. bribery case
content: The penalty payment comes as U.S. prosecutors are reported to be set to drop charges against Mr. Adani

title: M.P. BJP leader loses post over large convoy despite PM’s austerity appeal
content: In a letter dated May 14, Kisan Morcha’s State unit chief Jaipal Chavda said that Mr. Yadav’s appointment has been cancelled on directions of M.P. BJP chief Hemant Khandelwal

title: Former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan slams Centre for hiking fuel prices
content: He said the Centre had used the rise of crude oil prices owing to the crisis in West Asia to fleece ordinary people, who would feel the bite of the fuel price hike acutely

title: Senior IPS officer Davidson Devasirvatham posted in Tamil Nadu Cooperative Milk Producers Federation
content: The officer who was on ‘compulsory wait’ has been posted as DGP/ Chief Vigilance Officer in TMCMPF Limited, Chennai.

content: It is one thing to say that he is the leader of Malayali masses; but a bit of unpacking of that reveals the community dynamics at play

title: Nainar Nagenthran questions Vijay’s statement on minority government
content: Mr. Nagenthran claimed that the DMK now sits in the Opposition because, when it was in power, it insulted the sentiments of Hindus.

title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked by ₹3 across all variants
content: The latest price hikes extend to both the regular variants of petrol and diesel alongside the premium and high-octane products

title: Fuel price surge jolts Kerala as people brace for all-round price rise
content: Indian Oil Corporation appears more lenient, allowing dealers to pay for a load already delivered before receiving the next within two or three days. The other two companies, however, insist on advance payment, said a petroleum dealer

title: Watch: Rathindra Bose elected Speaker of West Bengal Assembly unopposed
content: BJP MLA Rathindra Bose was elected unopposed as the Speaker of the West Bengal assembly on Friday, becoming the first legislator from the northern part of the state to hold the post. His name was proposed in the House by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, following which pro tem Speaker Tapas Roy conducted a voice vote.

title: National Testing Agency announces NEET-UG re-exam on June 21
content: On May 12, the NTA had announced the cancellation of NEET-UG and said the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) would carry out a comprehensive inquiry into the alleged irregularities linked to the exam

title: Aravalli row: Won't pass order in favour of mining lease holders for now, says Supreme Court
content: "Lot of things are happening there. We are getting feedback and it is quite disturbing," the CJI observed

title: Street dogs eat amputated limb in Vijayawada government hospital; probe ordered
content: NTR district Collector Lakshmisha has ordered inquiry into the incident, and directed the GGH authorities to take action against the negligent staff

title: UDF leadership meeting to lay out the contours of the next Kerala cabinet gets underway
content: The UDF is seeking to turn the page on the uncertainty that gripped the alliance following the Congress’s ten-day delay in choosing its Chief Ministerial candidate by fast-tracking the government formation process.

title: V.D. Satheesan vows to cushion cost of living crisis exacerbated by fuel price hike in Kerala
content: The new State government might consider partial work-from-home options for public offices and the private sector to help employees save on increasing commuting costs

title: Rose Show inaugurated in Nilgiris
content: The Rose Show is to take place till May 18. The rose garden currently hosts 4,301 varieties of rose plants, out of which more than 3,300 are in full bloom. QR codes installed near each bird display for visitors to scan, hear the sounds and learn more details about the birds.

title: Municipal finances: 43 cities show low own revenue, limited borrowing, says Praja study
content: A study of 43 municipal corporations across 28 States and two Union Territories has found that cities in India depend on government grants for nearly 60% of their income

title: Major reason for economic crisis in India is Modi Government’s leadership crisis: Kharge after fuel hike
content: The Congress president said when diesel prices rise, inflation has a cascading effect across the country, adversely affecting everyone — from industries to household budgets and farmers

title: OMCs forced to increase prices of petrol, diesel: Andhra Minister Nadendla Manohar
content: Nadendla Manohar said, “To meet the crisis, Andhra government is taking steps to increase ethanol blending and encouraging measures like car pooling”

title: AIADMK split: Velumani faction willing to hold talks with Palaniswami on intra-party issues
content: Referring to Mr. Palaniswami’s statement that he was ready to make any sacrifice, Mr. Velumani said that the AIADMK general secretary should invite his faction for discussion, at least once

title: Jaishankar, Iran’s Araghchi discuss West Asia crisis, energy security and maritime stability
content: Discussed the situation in West Asia and its implications. Also exchanged views on bilateral issues of mutual interest, says External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar

content: Chennai-based Arun Loganathan, inspired by a Tamil movie starring Vijay, has contributed towards ‘fulfilling his individual debt responsibility’

content: Pune police on May 14 said that the CCTV footage shows the suspect arriving on a moped at the hospital before planting the device. “We have been scrutinising the footage to identify and trace the suspect

content: The decision to sing the full version of Vande Mataram at government events has revived debate over the song’s communal undertones and its compatibility with the secular and multicultural foundations of the Indian state

content: Tucked away in Mayurbhanj district in Odisha, The Belgadia Palace champions the region’s art and culture, while empowering local communities. We check in, draped in marigold garlands and woven shawls

content: Certain officials of IDFC First Bank and AU Small Finance Bank, in connivance with public servants of various departments of the Haryana government, allegedly misappropriated government funds through fraudulent means, an official statement said

title: Farmers’ body, Opposition seek immediate rollback of sugar export ban
content: The All India Kisan Sabha said that the government’s decision to ban sugar import is slated to have an adverse impact on the sugar industry in Maharashtra and across the country

title: Goa Congress leader Ketan Bhatikar dies of snakebite
content: The Goa Pradesh Congress Committee paid tribute to its party leader and extended condolences to the bereaved family, as well as his friends and supporters

title: Rathindra Bose, a Chartered Accountant from north Bengal, elected Speaker of Bengal Assembly
content: This is the first time that a leader from north Bengal has been selected for the Speaker’s post; Mr. Bose is the MLA from the Cooch Behar Dakshin Assembly seat, where he defeated Trinamool Congress’s Avijit De Bhaumik

title: Trinamool Congress leader Derek O’Brien hits out at Centre over fuel price hike
content: "First they loot your vote, then they kick you where it hurts. Pathetically predictable," Derek O'Brien said

content: Mr Chennithala also appeared to dispel reports that he would eschew joining Mr Satheesan’s cabinet. When asked whether he would join the government as a Minister, Mr Chennithala said, “Forming the cabinet is the Chief Minister’s prerogative”

content: According to a notification, the Commission shall submit its report to the Centre "as soon as possible but not later than November, 20, 2026”

title: Fuel delivery delays spark concerns across northern Kerala districts
content: Dealers in Kozhikode, Wayanad and Malappuram districts say many outlets are facing unforeseen disruption in fuel supply, leading to uncertainty over regular operations. They also claim that supply restrictions have been in place soon after reports emerged about the increase in fuel prices.

content: In Bulandshahr, the Neem River was revived through a 29-km intervention under MGNREGA. Officials said farmers voluntarily cleared encroachments

content: VD Satheesan's response came a day after the BJP said that he was selected as the Kerala chief minister following the influence of the IUML and Jamaat-e-Islami.

content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

content: Four family members died when their house roof collapsed due to heavy rain in Karnataka's Vijayapura district.

title: Present set-up lets govt pick CEC, ECs of its choice: SC
content: a panel of PM, a cabinet minister and the leader of opposition, pick the candidate . it said that an independent person should be made part of panel in place of minister . the bench of justices dipankar Gupta and Satish Chandra Sharma said that it was not necessary that the CJI participate in the process .

title: Satheesan named Kerala CM: Rahul spoke to Venugopal for nearly 2 hrs to avert bickering within party
content: V M Sudheeran was the last man the Gandhi scion is learnt to have heard . he met former KPCC chiefs and senior leaders on Tuesday afternoon . sources say he lost out in the final lap of the chat .
